title: Payroll App Unreachable After Last Night's ACL Change

ticket.summary: Since this morning, users in the Finance VLAN (10.40.8.0/23) cannot reach the payroll application at 172.16.20.45 on TCP 8443 — browsers show 'connection timed out'. Everything else works. Last night the security team pushed an ACL update to core-rtr1 to block a scanning host (10.40.9.66) from reaching the server segment.

topology.description: Finance clients (VLAN 80, 10.40.8.0/23) reach the server segment (172.16.20.0/24) through core-rtr1, which applies ACL SERVER-PROTECT inbound on the finance-facing interface. Last night's change intent was to block a single misbehaving host, 10.40.9.66, from the server segment.

logs.content:
Jul 16 23:41:05: %ARCHIVE-5-LOG: user sec-team config change, ticket SEC-1182 (block scanner 10.40.9.66)
+ip access-list extended SERVER-PROTECT
+ deny ip 10.40.8.0 0.0.1.255 172.16.20.0 0.0.0.255
+ permit ip any any

Jul 17 08:02:11: %SEC-6-IPACCESSLOGP: list SERVER-PROTECT denied tcp 10.40.8.31(51844) -> 172.16.20.45(8443), 1 packet
Jul 17 08:02:44: %SEC-6-IPACCESSLOGP: list SERVER-PROTECT denied tcp 10.40.8.90(52011) -> 172.16.20.45(8443), 1 packet
Jul 17 08:03:19: %SEC-6-IPACCESSLOGP: list SERVER-PROTECT denied tcp 10.40.9.12(49230) -> 172.16.20.45(8443), 1 packet

cli_outputs.output:
Extended IP access list SERVER-PROTECT
    10 deny ip 10.40.8.0 0.0.1.255 172.16.20.0 0.0.0.255 (14822 matches)
    20 permit ip any any (982133 matches)

ip access-list extended SERVER-PROTECT
interface GigabitEthernet0/1
 ip access-group SERVER-PROTECT in

monitoring.notes: The ACL-deny counter on core-rtr1 for SERVER-PROTECT was flat at ~0/min for months, then jumped to hundreds of denies per minute starting exactly at last night's 23:41 change window. Denied sources span the entire 10.40.8.0/23 finance subnet — not just the intended scanner at 10.40.9.66 — confirming the deny entry matches far more than one host.

hints: The change intent was to block one host (10.40.9.66), but the deny log shows sources from all over 10.40.8.0/23 — compare the intent with what the ACL entry actually matches.
Look at the wildcard mask on the deny entry in 'show ip access-lists SERVER-PROTECT'.
The entry 'deny ip 10.40.8.0 0.0.1.255 ...' blocks the whole /23, not the single scanner host — it should have been 'deny ip host 10.40.9.66 172.16.20.0 0.0.0.255'.

solution.root_cause: Last night's ACL change (SEC-1182) added 'deny ip 10.40.8.0 0.0.1.255 172.16.20.0 0.0.0.255' to SERVER-PROTECT — a wildcard mask covering the entire finance /23 — instead of denying only the scanning host 10.40.9.66. Every finance client is now denied access to the server segment, including the payroll app at 172.16.20.45.
solution.fix: Replace the overly-broad deny with a host-specific entry: 'deny ip host 10.40.9.66 172.16.20.0 0.0.0.255' (followed by the existing permit), so only the scanner is blocked and finance traffic to the payroll app is restored.
